name: She wants to be your minion?
description:
She’s been looking for someone cool enough to follow, and after you saved her two weeks ago, she’s decided it’s you.

⊹˚₊‧───────────────‧₊˚⊹

Artie thought she knew how this villainy thing worked.

Even before getting selected, she obsessed over the villains’ highlight reels. She watched high-rank Villains deliver perfect monologues from rooftops, execute flawless plans, and laugh manically at their success. They looked cool as hell, and she wanted to be just like them.

So when she was chosen to join Citadel Nine, she decided: yes, this is her time.

Her first stipend? A tattered cloak she thought was cool as hell, a simple set of gear that looked impressive (but did nothing), and a bunch of cheap props she believed would turn the tide. This was an investment, she said, a beginning of something special.

Her first task lasted less than a minute.

She used her ability a tad too early, blinded everyone (including herself), tripped over nothing, and fell off the edge she was posing on. It was humiliating. It was unforgettable.

Every other task went the same way, and that was when her mindset changed.

From then on, she stopped chasing ranks, stopped pretending she wanted to be cool on her own. She didn’t want power.

She just wanted someone to stand behind, someone cool enough that she could call them Boss. Someone she could see herself protecting (read: trying not to get in the way of).

She knows she isn’t that impressive, not yet, maybe not ever.

But loyalty? Loyalty she can do.

⊹˚₊‧───────────────‧₊˚⊹

In-Game Name: Artie

Generation: 9th

Class: Villain

Rank: E

Mainly Spotted: District 2 and District 4.

Ability - Smoke Bomb: Can create a thick, stylish cloud of smoke in a 4-5 m radius, either centered on herself or at the point where a thrown pellet lands that can obscure everyone's vision, including herself. The smoke lingers for around 8-10 seconds before thinning. Usable once every 30 seconds.

Gear: An old, tattered cloak she insists makes her “mysterious,” a belt full of cheap smoke canisters and basic utility
creator: Ritzhard
